assumptions: Conditional mean independence: E[u|X] = 0
No perfect collinearity
For valid inference: homoskedastic errors (relax with robust='hc1'/'hc3')
For cluster-robust SEs: enough clusters (≥ 30–50) and no cross-cluster dependence
description: OLS regression with robust/clustered standard errors. The workhorse of econometric analysis.

failure_modes.remedy: Drop collinear regressors or check dummy-variable coding.
failure_modes.symptom: Singular design / LinAlgError

failure_modes.remedy: Re-estimate with robust='hc1' (or 'hc3' for n < 250).
failure_modes.symptom: Heteroskedasticity test rejects (sp.het_test)

failure_modes.remedy: Use wild cluster bootstrap (sp.wild_cluster_bootstrap) or CR3 adjustment.
failure_modes.symptom: Few clusters (< 30) with cluster-robust SEs

name: regress
pre_conditions: data is a pandas DataFrame with every variable in formula as a column
outcome is numeric; non-numeric regressors should be categorical (handled via patsy)

assumptions: Relevance: instruments predict the endogenous regressor (first-stage F ≥ 10 rule of thumb)
Exclusion: instruments affect outcome only through the endogenous regressor
Monotonicity (for LATE interpretation under heterogeneous effects)
description: Unified IV estimation: 2SLS, LIML, Fuller, GMM, JIVE. Includes first-stage F, Sargan/Hansen J, and Hausman diagnostics.
example: sp.iv("wage ~ (education ~ parent_edu + distance) + experience", data=df, method="liml")

failure_modes.remedy: Use weak-IV-robust inference (Anderson-Rubin) or LIML.
failure_modes.symptom: First-stage F < 10 (Stock-Yogo 5% bias)

failure_modes.remedy: At least one instrument is invalid; drop instruments or switch to just-identified LIML.
failure_modes.symptom: Over-identification test rejects (sp.estat 'overid')

failure_modes.remedy: OLS may be consistent and more efficient; report both.
failure_modes.symptom: Hausman endogeneity test fails to reject

failure_modes.remedy: Use LIML or JIVE which are robust to many weak instruments.
failure_modes.symptom: Many instruments (≥ 10) cause many-IV bias

name: iv
pre_conditions: formula includes the (endog ~ instruments) parenthesised block
at least as many instruments as endogenous regressors (order condition)
instruments are not themselves endogenous in the outcome equation

signature.description: Unified IV estimation: 2SLS, LIML, Fuller, GMM, JIVE. Includes first-stage F, Sargan/Hansen J, and Hausman diagnostics. Validation: certified parity evidence.

assumptions: Conditional quantile is linear in covariates
Outcome continuous (or finely discretized) so the check-function minimization is well-posed
Powell (1991) sandwich SEs require a consistent kernel density of the conditional density at zero
description: Quantile regression at specified quantile(s).

failure_modes.symptom: Crossing/degenerate fit when regressors are collinear

assumptions: Count outcome with overdispersion: NB2 variance mu+alpha*mu^2 or NB1 variance mu*(1+delta), with alpha>0
Log-linear conditional mean exp(x'beta)
Observations conditionally independent given covariates (or correctly clustered)
description: Negative-binomial count regression for overdispersed non-negative outcomes. Formula fixed effects such as 'y ~ x | id' are implemented with explicit dummies for moderate panels.
example: sp.nbreg("visits ~ age + income | person", data=df, cluster="person")

failure_modes.remedy: Refit with Poisson since NB reduces to Poisson at alpha=0
failure_modes.symptom: Estimated alpha collapses toward 0 (no overdispersion)

failure_modes.remedy: Rescale covariates, raise maxiter, or simplify the linear predictor
failure_modes.symptom: ML fails to converge with extreme/separated counts

failure_modes.remedy: Use a zero-inflated or hurdle count model for the structural-zero process
failure_modes.symptom: Excess zeros beyond NB dispersion produces poor fit

name: nbreg
pre_conditions: Non-negative integer count outcome
Overdispersion present (else Poisson suffices)
reference: Cameron & Trivedi (2013) Regression Analysis of Count Data

signature.description: Negative-binomial count regression for overdispersed non-negative outcomes. Formula fixed effects such as 'y ~ x | id' are implemented with explicit dummies for moderate panels. Validation: certified parity evidence.

formula.description: Formula such as 'count ~ x1 + x2 | id'

assumptions: Panel count overdispersion (NB-2): model='fe' adds explicit entity dummies, model='re' is a random-intercept NB model
Log-linear conditional mean within panel units
RE random intercepts are normally distributed and independent of covariates
description: Panel negative-binomial regression. model='fe' uses explicit entity fixed effects through nbreg; model='re' dispatches to the random-intercept NB-2 GLMM menbreg.
example: sp.xtnbreg("visits ~ age + income", data=df, entity="person", model="fe")

failure_modes.remedy: Require multiple within-unit observations or switch to the RE model
failure_modes.symptom: FE with many singleton units inflates dummy count and degrades estimates

failure_modes.remedy: Increase quadrature points/maxiter or use model='fe'
failure_modes.symptom: RE model quadrature fails to converge

failure_modes.remedy: Pass entity= or include '| id' in the formula
failure_modes.symptom: Entity identifier missing for fixed effects

description: Unified synthetic control estimator. method= selects variant: 'classic', 'demeaned', 'detrended', 'unconstrained', 'elastic_net', 'augmented', 'sdid', 'gsynth', 'staggered'. inference= selects: 'placebo', 'conformal', 'bootstrap', 'jackknife'.
example: sp.synth(data=df, outcome="gdp", unit="state", time="year", treated_unit="CA", treatment_time=1989, method="demeaned")

failure_modes.remedy: Poor pre-fit — switch to method='demeaned'/'augmented' or enlarge donor pool.
failure_modes.symptom: Pre-treatment RMSPE > post-treatment effect

failure_modes.remedy: Use inference='conformal' (valid under weak assumptions) or report ranked placebo statistic.
failure_modes.symptom: Placebo p-value ≥ 0.1 despite visible gap

failure_modes.remedy: Interpolation bias risk — check method='elastic_net' or augmented SCM.
failure_modes.symptom: All weight concentrated on one donor

failure_modes.remedy: Extrapolation needed — use method='unconstrained' or 'augmented'.
failure_modes.symptom: Treated unit outside donor convex hull

failure_modes.remedy: S-Learner regularization smooths treatment coefficient toward zero; use T/X/DR instead.
failure_modes.symptom: S-Learner estimates near zero regardless of true effect

failure_modes.remedy: X-Learner needs well-identified control-outcome model; fall back to T-Learner or weighted T-Learner.
failure_modes.symptom: X-Learner fails when treated group is very small

assumptions: Static FE: strict exogeneity of regressors conditional on unit fixed effects (E[u_it | x_i, alpha_i] = 0).
Random effects: unit effect uncorrelated with regressors; relax with Mundlak / Chamberlain.
Dynamic GMM: weak exogeneity and no second-order serial correlation in differenced errors.
Enough clusters (>= 30-50) for cluster-robust SEs to be valid.
description: Unified panel regression: FE, RE, between, FD, pooled OLS, two-way FE, Mundlak/Chamberlain CRE, Arellano-Bond, Blundell-Bond system GMM. Results include built-in diagnostics: .hausman_test(), .bp_lm_test(), .f_test_effects(), .pesaran_cd_test(), .compare(method).
example: sp.panel(df, "wage ~ edu + exp", entity="worker", time="year", method="fe")

failure_modes.remedy: Switch to fixed effects (method='fe') or correlated random effects (method='mundlak').
failure_modes.symptom: Hausman test rejects RE

failure_modes.remedy: Use wild-cluster bootstrap or CR2/CR3 small-sample corrections.
failure_modes.symptom: Few clusters (< 30) inflate Type I error with cluster-robust SEs

failure_modes.remedy: Switch to method='hdfe' / feols for absorption, or drop singletons.
failure_modes.symptom: High-dimensional fixed effects make the design singular

assumptions: Cinelli-Hazlett (2020) omitted-variable sensitivity in the partial-R-squared framework; the Robustness Value is the minimum partial R-squared a confounder needs with both treatment and outcome to nullify the estimate.

assumptions: Simonsohn-Simmons-Nelson (2020) specification-curve: each combination of controls, SE type, sub-sample, and outcome transform is a defensible specification of the same x->y effect.
The set of analytical choices is researcher-enumerated and is assumed to span the reasonable garden of forking paths, not just confirmatory ones.
The curve summarizes effect stability across specifications; it does not by itself license a causal interpretation of any single fit.
Clustered SEs require a valid cluster_var aligned with the data.

assumptions: Correct specification of the spatial process (SAR vs. SEM vs. SDM): mis-specification biases all coefficients.
Spatial weights matrix W is exogenous and known.
Errors are i.i.d. (use SARAR / SAC if spatial error correlation is suspected).
Stationarity: (I - rho * W) is invertible.
description: Spatial Autoregressive (Lag) Model: Y = ρWY + Xβ + ε via ML.

failure_modes.remedy: Check W normalization and isolated units; bound rho away from boundary.
failure_modes.symptom: Log-likelihood fails to maximize / eigenvalue extreme

failure_modes.remedy: Switch to SAC / SDM, or test SEM specification (sp.sem).
failure_modes.symptom: Moran's I on residuals still rejects spatial randomness

name: sar
pre_conditions: Spatial weights matrix W is N x N and matches data row order.
W is typically row-normalized (so rho lies in (-1, 1) for stationarity).
No isolated units (no zero rows in W).
Cross-section size N >= 50 for ML asymptotics to bite.

signature.description: Spatial Autoregressive (Lag) Model: Y = ρWY + Xβ + ε via ML. Validation: validated.

data.description: pandas DataFrame containing the variables used by the estimator.

assumptions: Spatial dependence in the error term only (no spatial lag of y in the structural equation).
Lambda parameter in (-1, 1) for stationarity.
Correct specification: misclassifying as SEM when SAR / SDM hold induces bias.
description: Spatial Error Model: Y = Xβ + u, u = λWu + ε via ML.

failure_modes.remedy: Switch to SDM (Spatial Durbin Model) which nests SEM under a parameter restriction.
failure_modes.symptom: Common-factor test rejects

name: sem
pre_conditions: Spatial weights matrix W is N x N and matches data rows.
Residual spatial autocorrelation is the suspected concern (otherwise consider SAR / SDM).

signature.description: Spatial Error Model: Y = Xβ + u, u = λWu + ε via ML. Validation: validated.

data.description: pandas DataFrame containing the variables used by the estimator.

assumptions: Observations (or clusters/blocks) are exchangeable under the resampling scheme used; cluster= resamples whole clusters, block= preserves within-block ordering for serial dependence.
The statistic callable is a smooth, well-defined functional of the data so its sampling distribution is approximable by resampling.
BCa intervals further assume an estimable bias/acceleration; percentile/normal CIs assume approximate pivotality of the resampled distribution.
description: General bootstrap inference: nonparametric, cluster, block. Percentile/BCa/normal CIs.

failure_modes.remedy: Use a wild cluster bootstrap with Rademacher weights when the number of clusters is small (<~40).
failure_modes.symptom: Few clusters yields anti-conservative CIs / wildly unstable bootstrap distribution

failure_modes.remedy: Make the statistic robust to missing categories/empty strata or wrap to skip degenerate resamples.

assumptions: Existence of an outcome bridge function h(w, a, x) that recovers E[Y(a) | U, X]
Z and W are conditionally independent given U and (A, X)
Z ⊥ Y | U, A, X (exclusion on Z)
W ⊥ A | U, X (exclusion on W)
Z is relevant for W given A, X (bridge first stage)
description: Proximal Causal Inference via linear 2SLS on the outcome bridge. Identifies ATE with unmeasured confounding using two proxy variables: a treatment-side Z (instrument for W) and an outcome-side W (endogenous bridge regressor).

failure_modes.remedy: Try richer Z or more proxies; without first-stage strength the bridge is underidentified.
failure_modes.symptom: First-stage (Z → W) too weak

failure_modes.remedy: Proxy variation insufficient — redesign measurement or fall back to sensitivity (sp.sensemakr).
failure_modes.symptom: Proxies collapse to nearly-constant

failure_modes.remedy: Report multiple bridge families; compare with sp.negative_control_outcome / _exposure.
failure_modes.symptom: Estimate highly sensitive to bridge specification

name: proximal
pre_conditions: at least one treatment-side proxy Z (independent of outcome given U, X)
at least one outcome-side proxy W (independent of treatment given U, X)
proxy_z and proxy_w measure the same unmeasured confounder U from different angles
n ≥ 1000 — 2SLS on proxies is noisy
reference: Tchetgen Tchetgen et al. (2020); Miao, Geng & Tchetgen Tchetgen (2018)

assumptions: Monotonicity (no defiers) for method='monotonicity'
Principal ignorability for method='principal_score' (strata ⊥ Y(d) | X)
SUTVA and exclusion restriction for the never-takers / always-takers interpretation
Overlap in the principal score when method='principal_score'
description: Principal Stratification (Frangakis & Rubin 2002). 'monotonicity' method identifies the complier PCE (= LATE) and reports Zhang-Rubin sharp bounds on the always-survivor SACE. 'principal_score' uses Ding-Lu covariate weighting to point-identify stratum-specific effects under principal ignorability.

failure_modes.remedy: Strata partition too weak for point identification — add covariates and use method='principal_score'.
failure_modes.symptom: Zhang-Rubin bounds include 0 and both signs

failure_modes.remedy: Low compliance — report only bounds; LATE SE explodes.
failure_modes.symptom: Complier share near zero

failure_modes.remedy: Principal-score inversion is unstable — restrict to overlap region or fall back to method='monotonicity'.
failure_modes.symptom: Principal score fails overlap

limitations: Always-survivor SACE under encouragement design (Mealli & Pacini 2013, partial identification) is not yet implemented; only AIR / Wald LATE point estimates (τ_Y on outcome, τ_S on the post-treatment stratum) are reported when an instrument is supplied
name: principal_strat
pre_conditions: binary treatment
binary post-treatment stratum variable (compliance, survival, employment, …)
covariates required when method='principal_score' (for Ding-Lu weighting)
n ≥ 300 per (treat × stratum) cell for stable bounds
reference: Frangakis & Rubin (2002); Zhang & Rubin (2003); Ding & Lu (2017)

signature.description: Principal Stratification (Frangakis & Rubin 2002). 'monotonicity' method identifies the complier PCE (= LATE) and reports Zhang-Rubin sharp bounds on the always-survivor SACE. 'principal_score' uses Ding-Lu covariate weighting to point-identify stratum-specific effects under principal ignorability. Validation: validated. Known limitations: Always-survivor SACE under encouragement design (Mealli & Pacini 2013, partial identification) is not yet implemented; only AIR / Wald LATE point estimates (τ_Y on outcome, τ_S on the post-treatment stratum) are reported when an instrument is supplied.
signature.name: principal_strat

assumptions: Invariance: the conditional distribution of Y given its true direct causes is stable across all environments (Peters et al. 2016)
Environments perturb predictors but do not intervene directly on Y and do not change the Y|causes mechanism
Linear-Gaussian mechanism under method='linear' (F-test on residuals); method='nonlinear' relaxes to a K-S test on local-linear residuals
Returns the intersection of invariant predictor subsets — yields a conservative (possibly empty) set of guaranteed direct parents
description: Invariant Causal Prediction: infer direct parents of Y by testing invariance of P(Y | X_S) across environments.

failure_modes.remedy: Provide environment with at least two distinct integer codes; more environments sharpen identification.
failure_modes.symptom: fewer than two environments supplied — no invariance contrast possible

failure_modes.remedy: Relax alpha, switch to method='nonlinear', or check whether an environment directly perturbs Y.
failure_modes.symptom: no subset is invariant across environments, so the accepted parent set is empty

name: icp
pre_conditions: Predictor matrix X (n x p) and response y of length n
An integer-coded environment label of length n with at least two distinct environments

signature.description: Invariant Causal Prediction: infer direct parents of Y by testing invariance of P(Y | X_S) across environments. Validation: validated.
signature.name: icp

assumptions: The selection diagram correctly encodes distribution shifts.
Transport formula validity depends on the supplied graph.
description: Pearl-Bareinboim transportability: enumerate s-admissible adjustment sets on a selection diagram; returns the transport formula or NOT identifiable.

failure_modes.remedy: Find additional adjustment variables or report non-transportability.
failure_modes.symptom: Selection node opens an unblocked path to the outcome

name: identify_transport
pre_conditions: Source/target selection node and causal graph are specified.
Treatment and outcome nodes exist in the graph.

signature.description: Pearl-Bareinboim transportability: enumerate s-admissible adjustment sets on a selection diagram; returns the transport formula or NOT identifiable. Validation: validated.

assumptions: Sequential ignorability / no unmeasured confounding in the logged data
Positivity: logging policy assigns positive probability to every action the target policy can take
Logging propensities are correctly specified (IPS/SNIPS/DR weights are valid)
description: Container returned by sp.ope.* estimators (IPS, SNIPS, DR, Switch-DR, DM). Reports value, SE, CI, importance-ratio diagnostics.

failure_modes.remedy: Read the estimator's documented OPEResult fields; use the policy_learning OPEResult subclass when you need the estimator attribute
failure_modes.symptom: Accessing a field that the chosen estimator did not populate

failure_modes.remedy: Switch to a self-normalised or doubly-robust estimator and inspect effective sample size
failure_modes.symptom: Huge variance / unstable value estimate from near-zero logging propensities

name: OPEResult
pre_conditions: Produced by an off-policy-evaluation estimator (ips / snips / doubly_robust / direct_method) — not constructed directly
Logged (action, reward, propensity) tuples underlie the fitted estimator

signature.description: Container returned by sp.ope.* estimators (IPS, SNIPS, DR, Switch-DR, DM). Reports value, SE, CI, importance-ratio diagnostics. Validation: validated.

assumptions: Latent-confounder model: a low-dimensional latent generates the observed proxies, treatment, and outcome (Louizos et al. 2017)
Proxy sufficiency — observed covariates carry enough signal to recover the confounder
Correct VAE specification (treatment/outcome likelihoods and prior) and adequate optimisation
description: Causal Effect Variational Auto-Encoder: infer a latent confounder Z from noisy proxies X, then estimate ITE via counterfactual decoding. Uses PyTorch when available, else a numpy linear-variational fallback.

failure_modes.remedy: Install the neural extra: pip install 'StatsPAI[neural]'
failure_modes.symptom: torch not installed when invoking the neural CEVAE backend

failure_modes.remedy: Increase epochs, lower the learning rate, or add more proxy covariates; validate against a regression baseline
failure_modes.symptom: Variational lower bound fails to converge or posterior collapses, yielding biased effect estimates

name: cevae
pre_conditions: torch installed (neural extra) for the variational autoencoder backend
Individual-level data with treatment, outcome, and proxy covariates

outcome.description: Outcome variable column name or outcome array.

seed.description: Random seed for reproducible stochastic steps.

treatment.description: Treatment indicator, treatment variable, or treatment array.

assumptions: The 2x2 table cross-classifies a binary exposure and binary outcome on independent observations.
The Woolf CI relies on asymptotic normality of log(OR), adequate when no cell is small.
The exact (Fisher conditional) CI assumes fixed margins under the non-central hypergeometric model.
description: Odds ratio from a 2x2 table with Woolf (asymptotic) or Fisher-exact CI. Haldane-Anscombe correction for zero cells.

failure_modes.remedy: Use method='exact' for a conditional CI that tolerates zero cells.
failure_modes.symptom: A zero cell makes the Woolf log-OR SE infinite or the OR 0/inf.

failure_modes.remedy: Use conditional logistic regression for matched 2x2 data.
failure_modes.symptom: Matched/clustered design analyzed as unmatched, biasing the OR.

name: odds_ratio
pre_conditions: Four non-negative 2x2 cell counts (a,b,c,d) or a 2x2 array, laid out exposed/unexposed x outcome+/-.
Observations are independent (not matched pairs or repeated measures).

signature.description: Odds ratio from a 2x2 table with Woolf (asymptotic) or Fisher-exact CI. Haldane-Anscombe correction for zero cells. Validation: validated.
signature.name: odds_ratio

alpha.description: Significance level for confidence intervals and tests.

method.description: CI method

assumptions: The 2x2 table comes from a cohort or cross-sectional design where outcome risk (not just odds) is directly estimable.
Observations are independent within each exposure group.
The Katz log-RR CI assumes large-sample normality; the Haldane 0.5 correction is applied only when a cell is zero.
description: Relative risk (risk ratio) from a 2x2 table with Katz log-RR CI. Haldane correction for zero cells.

failure_modes.remedy: Report the odds ratio for case-control designs instead of RR.
failure_modes.symptom: Applied to case-control data where baseline risk is not sampled, so RR is not identified.

failure_modes.remedy: Report the corrected estimate cautiously or fit a log-binomial/Poisson model.
failure_modes.symptom: Zero events in a group triggers the Haldane correction and a wide, approximate CI.

name: relative_risk
pre_conditions: 2x2 counts with exposed/unexposed rows and outcome+/- columns from a cohort or cross-sectional sample.
Denominators (row totals) are positive in both exposure groups.

signature.description: Relative risk (risk ratio) from a 2x2 table with Katz log-RR CI. Haldane correction for zero cells. Validation: validated.
signature.name: relative_risk

alpha.description: Significance level for confidence intervals and tests.

assumptions: The 2x2 table is from a cohort or cross-sectional design so absolute risks per group are estimable.
Observations are independent within each exposure arm.
The Wald CI assumes normality (poor near risk 0 or 1); Newcombe's hybrid-score CI corrects the boundary overshoot.
description: Risk difference (absolute risk reduction) with Wald or Newcombe hybrid-score CI.

failure_modes.remedy: Use method='newcombe' for a boundary-respecting score interval.
failure_modes.symptom: Wald CI overshoots below -1 or above 1 when a proportion is near a boundary.

failure_modes.remedy: Increase sample size or report an exact interval; consider a ratio measure.
failure_modes.symptom: Very small per-group n yields an unstable, overly wide difference.

name: risk_difference
pre_conditions: 2x2 counts with exposed/unexposed rows and outcome+/- columns.
Both group denominators are positive.

signature.description: Risk difference (absolute risk reduction) with Wald or Newcombe hybrid-score CI. Validation: validated.
signature.name: risk_difference

alpha.description: Significance level for confidence intervals and tests.

method.description: Estimator or algorithm variant to use.

assumptions: The supplied or estimated RR is causal and unconfounded, so AF_exposed = (RR-1)/RR validly attributes excess risk to exposure.
Exposure prevalence P_e is measured in the target population to which the PAF is generalized.
The delta-method CI on log(1-PAF) assumes large-sample normality, which degrades when PAF is near 0 or 1.
description: Attributable fractions in the exposed (AF) and in the population (Levin PAF) with delta-method CI.

failure_modes.remedy: For protective exposures report a prevented fraction instead, or recode so the harmful category is 'exposed'.
failure_modes.symptom: RR < 1 (protective exposure) yields a negative attributable fraction that is misinterpreted as preventable burden.

failure_modes.remedy: Clamp exposure prevalence to a proportion and pass a strictly positive RR.
failure_modes.symptom: P_e outside [0,1] or RR <= 0 passed in.

name: attributable_risk
pre_conditions: A risk ratio (RR > 0) and an exposure prevalence P_e in [0,1], or 2x2 counts from which RR is derived.
RR and P_e refer to the same population and outcome definition.

signature.description: Attributable fractions in the exposed (AF) and in the population (Levin PAF) with delta-method CI. Validation: validated.
signature.name: attributable_risk

alpha.description: Significance level for confidence intervals and tests.

assumptions: Events in each arm are Poisson over person-time at risk with a constant underlying rate (no time-varying hazard within group).
Person-time is measured in consistent units across the exposed and unexposed arms.
The exact CI uses the F-distribution/Poisson relationship, valid for count data without overdispersion.
description: Person-time incidence rate ratio with exact Poisson CI (Clopper-Pearson on conditional binomial).

failure_modes.remedy: Use method='exact' which handles zero-event arms via the Poisson exact CI.
failure_modes.symptom: Zero events in one arm makes the Wald log-rate SE undefined.

failure_modes.remedy: Fit a Poisson/negative-binomial rate model with an offset for person-time.
failure_modes.symptom: Overdispersed counts (clustering/recurrent events) make the Poisson CI too narrow.

name: incidence_rate_ratio
pre_conditions: Event counts and person-time totals for an exposed and an unexposed group.
Person-time values are strictly positive and on the same time scale.

signature.description: Person-time incidence rate ratio with exact Poisson CI (Clopper-Pearson on conditional binomial). Validation: validated.
signature.name: incidence_rate_ratio

alpha.description: Significance level for confidence intervals and tests.

method.description: Estimator or algorithm variant to use.

assumptions: The stratum-specific association (OR or RR) is constant across strata, so a single pooled measure is meaningful (test homogeneity separately).
Strata define confounder levels over which the measure is conditioned to remove confounding.
Sparse-data validity holds: the MH estimator is consistent even with small per-stratum counts but many strata.
description: Mantel-Haenszel pooled OR or RR across K strata, with Robins-Breslow-Greenland variance and Cochran's Q homogeneity check.

failure_modes.remedy: Run Breslow-Day for homogeneity and report stratum-specific estimates if it rejects.
failure_modes.symptom: Effect modification (OR/RR varies by stratum) makes the pooled estimate misleading.

failure_modes.remedy: Drop empty strata and ensure tables contain discordant cells.
failure_modes.symptom: All strata degenerate to zero MH numerator/denominator terms.

name: mantel_haenszel
pre_conditions: A (K,2,2) array of stratum tables laid out [[a,b],[c,d]] = exposure x outcome.
K >= 1 strata with at least one informative (non-zero-margin) table.

signature.description: Mantel-Haenszel pooled OR or RR across K strata, with Robins-Breslow-Greenland variance and Cochran's Q homogeneity check. Validation: validated.
signature.name: mantel_haenszel

alpha.description: Significance level for confidence intervals and tests.

assumptions: Under H0 the true odds ratio is constant across all K strata (homogeneity), against which the test contrasts observed vs MH-expected cells.
The chi-square reference distribution with K-1 df is asymptotic and requires adequately large stratum-specific cell counts.
Tarone's correction is applied so the statistic is consistent with the MH common-OR estimator used to compute expected cells.
description: Breslow-Day test for homogeneity of the odds ratio across strata, with Tarone correction.

failure_modes.remedy: Collapse or drop sparse strata, or rely on the MH estimator with its OR-homogeneity caveat noted.
failure_modes.symptom: Sparse strata with zero or near-zero cells make expected counts unstable and inflate the statistic.

failure_modes.remedy: Provide at least two strata, or compute a single-table odds ratio.
failure_modes.symptom: Only one stratum supplied so there is nothing to test for homogeneity.

name: breslow_day_test
pre_conditions: A (K,2,2) array of stratum 2x2 tables with K >= 2 strata.
Each stratum has non-degenerate margins (both exposure and outcome levels present).

signature.description: Breslow-Day test for homogeneity of the odds ratio across strata, with Tarone correction. Validation: validated.
signature.name: breslow_day_test

assumptions: Stratum-specific rates from the study population are applied to an external standard population's stratum weights, so within-stratum rates are the estimands of interest.
Event counts within each stratum are Poisson, which the delta-method SE on the weighted rate sum relies on.
Standard weights and study strata are aligned to the same stratum definitions and ordering.
description: Direct age/covariate standardization of a rate using external standard-population weights.

failure_modes.remedy: Collapse empty strata or ensure every stratum has positive person-time/population.
failure_modes.symptom: A stratum has zero population denominator producing a divide-by-zero rate.

failure_modes.remedy: Use indirect standardization (SMR) which is more stable with sparse study strata.
failure_modes.symptom: Very small event counts in some strata make the Poisson-delta SE unreliable.

name: direct_standardize
pre_conditions: events, population, and standard_weights are equal-length per-stratum vectors.
All populations/person-times are positive and standard_weights are non-negative (normalized internally).

signature.description: Direct age/covariate standardization of a rate using external standard-population weights. Validation: validated.
signature.name: direct_standardize

alpha.description: Significance level for confidence intervals and tests.

assumptions: Reference (standard) stratum-specific rates apply to the study population to form expected events; SMR = observed/expected.
Observed events follow a Poisson distribution, underpinning the exact (Byar/Garwood) CI.
Reference rates are estimated precisely enough to be treated as fixed (no propagation of reference-rate uncertainty).
description: Indirect standardization -> SMR (standardized morbidity / mortality ratio) with Garwood exact Poisson CI.

failure_modes.remedy: Aggregate strata or use direct standardization if study stratum rates are estimable.
failure_modes.symptom: Expected events near zero (rare outcome, small study pop) yields an unstable SMR with a huge CI.

failure_modes.remedy: Provide positive reference denominators for every stratum used.
failure_modes.symptom: A reference stratum has zero population so its reference rate is undefined.

name: indirect_standardize
pre_conditions: Study population sizes per stratum plus reference events and reference population per matching stratum.
Strata in the study and reference vectors correspond one-to-one with positive reference populations.

signature.description: Indirect standardization -> SMR (standardized morbidity / mortality ratio) with Garwood exact Poisson CI. Validation: validated.
signature.name: indirect_standardize

alpha.description: Significance level for confidence intervals and tests.

assumptions: Viewpoint scores in [0,1] reflect external expert judgement; the function aggregates but does not itself verify causal evidence.
Unscored viewpoints (None) are missing-at-random with respect to the aggregate, since they drop from both numerator and denominator.
Temporality and the other eight viewpoints are treated as exchangeable contributions to a mean score (no formal weighting of necessity).
description: Structured 9-viewpoint Bradford-Hill causal-assessment rubric with prerequisite check (temporality required) and narrative verdict.

failure_modes.remedy: Score at least one Bradford-Hill viewpoint before calling.
failure_modes.symptom: All viewpoints left as None gives an empty/undefined aggregate score.

failure_modes.remedy: Normalize each viewpoint to the 0/0.5/1 evidence scale before passing.
failure_modes.symptom: A score outside [0,1] (e.g. a raw effect size) is passed for a viewpoint.

name: bradford_hill
pre_conditions: Each provided viewpoint score is a number in [0,1] or None.
At least one viewpoint is scored (non-None) so the denominator is positive.

signature.description: Structured 9-viewpoint Bradford-Hill causal-assessment rubric with prerequisite check (temporality required) and narrative verdict. Validation: validated.
signature.name: bradford_hill

assumptions: Same as IVW for non-outlier instruments.
Outlier removal heuristic correctly identifies pleiotropic SNPs.
Relevance: SNPs predict exposure (F-statistic ≥ 10 per SNP or set-F)
Independence: SNPs ⊥ confounders of exposure-outcome
Exclusion restriction: SNPs affect outcome only through exposure (InSIDE for Egger; ≥ 50% valid for median; modal for mode-based)
Monotonicity when interpreting LATE on genetically-shifted subpopulation
description: MR-PRESSO global test + per-SNP outlier detection + outlier-corrected IVW estimate + distortion test.

failure_modes.remedy: Relax outlier threshold or use mode-based estimator (mr_mode) instead.
failure_modes.symptom: All SNPs flagged as outliers

failure_modes.remedy: Use weighted-median or mode-based estimator; report Egger intercept + I² as pleiotropy diagnostic.
failure_modes.symptom: Egger intercept p < 0.05 — directional pleiotropy

failure_modes.remedy: Heterogeneity across SNPs — run sp.mr_presso to detect/remove outliers.
failure_modes.symptom: Q-statistic rejects homogeneity (Cochran's Q p < 0.05)

failure_modes.remedy: Weak-IV bias in IVW — use debiased IVW or LAP-type estimator (sp.mr_lap).
failure_modes.symptom: Set-F < 10 (weak instruments in aggregate)

failure_modes.remedy: SNPs explain more outcome variance than exposure — direction of effect questionable.
failure_modes.symptom: Steiger test flags reverse causation

name: mr_presso
pre_conditions: Two-sample summary statistics with at least ~10 SNP instruments.
Sufficient computational budget for the global / outlier permutation procedure.
SNP-summary statistics for exposure and outcome aligned by SNP
beta_exposure / beta_outcome / se_exposure / se_outcome arrays of equal length
≥ 10 genetic instruments for reliable IVW/median/mode; ≥ 20 for robust Egger intercept
mvmr needs SNP × exposure associations matrix

signature.description: MR-PRESSO global test + per-SNP outlier detection + outlier-corrected IVW estimate + distortion test. Validation: validated.
signature.name: mr_presso

seed.description: Random seed for reproducible stochastic steps.

assumptions: Sequential exchangeability conditional on recorded history.
No structural positivity violations over treatment histories.
description: Unified longitudinal causal-effect estimator. Auto-routes to IPW (no time-varying confounders) / MSM (dynamic regime with time-varying confounders) / parametric g-formula ICE (static regime). Accepts a string DSL or callable for the treatment regime.
example: sp.longitudinal_analyze(df, id='pid', time='visit', treatment='drug', outcome='cd4', time_varying=['cd4_lag'], regime='if cd4_lag < 200 then 1 else 0')

failure_modes.remedy: Coarsen histories, truncate weights, or switch to a simpler MSM/g-formula specification.
failure_modes.symptom: Sparse treatment histories or exploding weights

name: longitudinal_analyze
pre_conditions: Panel or person-period data identify unit, time, treatment, outcome, and covariate history.
reference: Hernan & Robins (2020) Causal Inference: What If

signature.description: Unified longitudinal causal-effect estimator. Auto-routes to IPW (no time-varying confounders) / MSM (dynamic regime with time-varying confounders) / parametric g-formula ICE (static regime). Accepts a string DSL or callable for the treatment regime. Validation: validated.
signature.name: longitudinal_analyze

assumptions: Sequential exchangeability (no unmeasured time-varying confounding) and positivity hold so each regime's mean outcome is identified via the underlying g-method.
The two regimes are well-defined dynamic/static treatment rules over the same time horizon.
The contrast SE uses an independence-style delta approximation, assuming negligible covariance between the two regime estimates.
description: Plug-in estimator of E[Y(regime_a)] - E[Y(regime_b)] with delta-method SE.

failure_modes.remedy: Choose less extreme regimes or check positivity of the treatment rules over follow-up.
failure_modes.symptom: One regime has near-zero effective support, giving an unstable or extreme mean

failure_modes.remedy: Use a joint/bootstrap variance over both regimes instead of the additive delta-method SE.
failure_modes.symptom: Reported contrast SE is too narrow because regime estimates are correlated

name: longitudinal_contrast
pre_conditions: Both regimes are evaluable on the long-format longitudinal data (matching covariate keys per period).
Time-varying confounders and treatment history are present for the g-method to adjust on.
Sufficient subjects following (or weighted toward) each regime to estimate both means.

signature.description: Plug-in estimator of E[Y(regime_a)] - E[Y(regime_b)] with delta-method SE. Validation: validated.
signature.name: longitudinal_contrast

assumptions: The rule is a deterministic function of observed history and period, encoding a static or dynamic treatment strategy.
DSL strings reference only available history columns and are restricted to safe expressions; the rule returns a valid treatment value each period.
K matches the intended number of decision points when rule is a scalar or sequence.
description: Build a dynamic or static treatment regime from a string DSL, list, callable, or scalar. Supports 'if <cond> then <a> else <b>', 'always_treat', 'never_treat', and arbitrary safe expressions. Parsed via a whitelisted AST walker — no dynamic code execution.

failure_modes.remedy: Reference only existing history columns and keep the rule to the documented safe-eval grammar.
failure_modes.symptom: DSL string references a missing covariate or uses unsupported syntax

failure_modes.remedy: Make the rule sequence length equal to K or pass a scalar/callable instead.
failure_modes.symptom: Sequence length disagrees with the number of periods K

name: regime
pre_conditions: History fields named in the rule exist when the Regime is later evaluated on data.
rule is one of the supported forms: str DSL, sequence, callable(h,t), or scalar.
K is set consistently with the longitudinal horizon being analyzed.

signature.description: Build a dynamic or static treatment regime from a string DSL, list, callable, or scalar. Run identify() before estimate() when the design is not obvious.

Auto-routes to IV / RD / DiD / longitudinal / selection-on-observables based on supplied fields. Validation: validated.

assumptions: y_true is an error-free gold standard against which y_pred is evaluated (no reference-test misclassification).
Sensitivity and specificity are intrinsic test properties assumed stable across the case/control spectrum.
Wilson-score CIs assume independent binary classifications.
description: Sensitivity, specificity, PPV, NPV, LR+ / LR- with Wilson score CIs.  Accepts either raw binary labels or pre-computed confusion counts.

failure_modes.remedy: Ensure the gold standard contains both classes; report Wilson CIs which handle small counts.
failure_modes.symptom: No true positives (or no true negatives) makes sensitivity (or specificity) undefined.

failure_modes.remedy: Use a true gold standard or latent-class correction and report the limitation.
failure_modes.symptom: Imperfect reference standard biases both metrics (verification/incorporation bias).

name: sensitivity_specificity
pre_conditions: Either equal-length binary y_true/y_pred vectors, or precomputed tp/fn/fp/tn confusion cells.
Both diseased (positives) and non-diseased (negatives) units are represented.

signature.description: Sensitivity, specificity, PPV, NPV, LR+ / LR- with Wilson score CIs.  Accepts either raw binary labels or pre-computed confusion counts. Validation: validated.

alpha.description: Significance level for confidence intervals and tests.

assumptions: y_true is a binary gold standard and scores are continuous with higher values indicating the positive class.
Observations are independent, so the Hanley-McNeil AUC SE (based on rank statistics) is valid.
Class labels are correctly specified (mislabeling inverts the curve below the diagonal).
description: ROC curve with AUC (trapezoidal) and Hanley-McNeil (1982) standard error.

failure_modes.remedy: Ensure both positive and negative cases are in y_true before computing the ROC.
failure_modes.symptom: Only one outcome class present so TPR/FPR and AUC are undefined.

failure_modes.remedy: Complement AUC with precision-recall or threshold-specific sensitivity/specificity.
failure_modes.symptom: Severe class imbalance makes AUC optimistic about clinical usefulness.

name: roc_curve
pre_conditions: y_true in {0,1} and a same-length continuous score vector.
Both classes (at least one positive and one negative) are present.

signature.description: ROC curve with AUC (trapezoidal) and Hanley-McNeil (1982) standard error. Validation: validated.

alpha.description: Significance level for confidence intervals and tests.

assumptions: The two raters classify the same units independently into a common, fixed set of categories.
Chance agreement is modeled from the product of each rater's marginal category frequencies (fixed-marginals assumption).
Linear/quadratic weights presume the category scale is genuinely ordinal with equally interpretable distances.
description: Cohen's kappa for inter-rater agreement on nominal or ordinal scales. Supports linear / quadratic weighting.

failure_modes.remedy: Report observed agreement alongside kappa and inspect the confusion matrix.
failure_modes.symptom: Highly imbalanced marginals (one category dominant) yield low kappa despite high raw agreement (the kappa paradox).

failure_modes.remedy: Align the two rating vectors to the same units before calling.
failure_modes.symptom: rater_a and rater_b have different lengths.

name: cohen_kappa
pre_conditions: rater_a and rater_b are equal-length sequences of category labels.
Both raters use the same category label set; for weighted kappa the categories are ordered.

signature.description: Cohen's kappa for inter-rater agreement on nominal or ordinal scales. Supports linear / quadratic weighting. Validation: validated.
signature.name: cohen_kappa

alpha.description: Significance level for confidence intervals and tests.

Loading is read-only and does not alter the registered estimand or analysis specification.

assumptions: Dashboard that dispatches every applicable sensitivity method (E-value, Cinelli-Hazlett RV, Oster's delta) to one fitted result.
Oster's delta and rho_max require both short- and long-regression R-squared (r2_treated, r2_controlled); rho_max bounds omitted-to-observed selection.
Each component inherits its own identifying assumptions (linear OVB for RV/Oster, RR scale for E-value); the panel does not reconcile conflicting ones.
The result must expose estimate, se, and ci for the methods to run.
description: Run every applicable sensitivity analysis in one shot: E-value, Oster delta (when R^2 inputs given), Rosenbaum Gamma (when matched structure exposed), Sensemakr (regression models), and a breakdown-frontier bias estimate. Also available as result.sensitivity().

failure_modes.remedy: Pass a fitted result exposing estimate, se, and ci rather than a raw scalar or DataFrame.
failure_modes.symptom: Result lacks estimate/se/ci, so no sensitivity component can be computed

failure_modes.remedy: Pass the short- and long-regression R-squared values to enable the Oster component.
failure_modes.symptom: Oster's delta omitted because r2_treated / r2_controlled were not provided

name: unified_sensitivity
pre_conditions: A result with point estimate, SE, and CI attributes
r2_treated and r2_controlled for the Oster's-delta component
reference: VanderWeele & Ding (2017); Oster (2019); Rosenbaum (2002); Cinelli & Hazlett (2020)

signature.description: Run every applicable sensitivity analysis in one shot: E-value, Oster delta (when R^2 inputs given), Rosenbaum Gamma (when matched structure exposed), Sensemakr (regression models), and a breakdown-frontier bias estimate. Also available as result.sensitivity(). Validation: validated.
signature.name: unified_sensitivity

assumptions: Surrogacy: conditional on surrogates and covariates the treatment has no direct effect on the long-term outcome (strictly stronger than ignorability)
Comparability / surrogate-stability: the conditional outcome mean estimated in the observational sample equals that conditional mean in the experimental sample
Common support of the surrogate (and covariate) distribution across the two samples
Treatment is randomised (or ignorable) in the experimental sample
description: Athey-Chetty-Imbens-Kang surrogate-index estimator for the long-term ATE: combines an experimental sample (treatment + short-term surrogate) with an observational sample (surrogate + long-term outcome) to extrapolate the effect on the long-term outcome.

failure_modes.remedy: Confirm every surrogate and covariate name exists in both experimental and observational frames
failure_modes.symptom: A surrogate or covariate column is absent from one of the two input frames

failure_modes.remedy: Defend surrogacy with a placebo long-term outcome in a validation sample or add more surrogates
failure_modes.symptom: Surrogacy fails (surrogate does not capture the full treatment->outcome path), biasing the long-term ATE

failure_modes.remedy: Restrict to the overlapping surrogate region or enrich the observational sample
failure_modes.symptom: Observational surrogate support does not cover the experimental distribution, so the conditional mean extrapolates

name: surrogate_index
pre_conditions: Experimental sample with treatment and surrogates (long-term outcome need not be present)
Observational sample with the same surrogates plus the long-term outcome
Surrogate (and optional covariate) columns shared by both samples
reference: Athey, Chetty, Imbens & Kang (2019). NBER WP 26463.

signature.description: Athey-Chetty-Imbens-Kang surrogate-index estimator for the long-term ATE: combines an experimental sample (treatment + short-term surrogate) with an observational sample (surrogate + long-term outcome) to extrapolate the effect on the long-term outcome. Validation: validated.
signature.name: surrogate_index

assumptions: Sequential surrogacy: each surrogate wave blocks the treatment's effect on later waves and on the long-term outcome (dynamic surrogacy)
Comparability across samples: the conditional outcome mean learned in the observational sample transports to the experimental sample for every wave
Each wave's surrogate columns are measured in both the experimental and observational samples
Treatment is sustained/long-term and the short-run waves capture its full downstream path
description: Long-term ATE under multi-wave short-term surrogates; extends the classical surrogate index to sustained treatments via iterated conditional expectations (Ghassami et al. 2024).

failure_modes.remedy: Ensure every wave column list is present in both experimental and observational frames
failure_modes.symptom: A wave's surrogate columns are missing from one of the two samples

failure_modes.remedy: Restrict waves to surrogates that fully mediate the effect, or validate with a placebo long-term outcome
failure_modes.symptom: Later waves are post-treatment colliders, breaking the iterated-expectation identification

failure_modes.remedy: Increase sample size or reduce the number of surrogate waves before bootstrapping
failure_modes.symptom: Too few units per treatment arm for stable bootstrap variance across K nested regressions

surrogates_waves given as an ordered list of per-wave column lists

signature.description: Long-term ATE under multi-wave short-term surrogates; extends the classical surrogate index to sustained treatments via iterated conditional expectations (Ghassami et al. 2024). Validation: validated.

assumptions: RCT and RWD estimates are each (conditionally) unbiased for the target estimand once the transport_shift is applied
Inverse-variance pooling assumes the two estimates are independent with correctly specified standard errors
transport_shift correctly transports the RCT estimate to the RWD target population, with transport_shift_se capturing its uncertainty (added in quadrature)
description: Inverse-variance pooling of an RCT and RWD estimate with optional transport shift (Dahabreh et al. 2020; arXiv:2511.19735 2025).

failure_modes.remedy: Pass positive finite SEs and weight_mode in {'inverse_variance','rct_heavy'}
failure_modes.symptom: Either supplied SE is non-positive or weight_mode is not a recognised option

failure_modes.remedy: Run a concordance check first and only pool when the two sources agree
failure_modes.symptom: RCT and RWD estimates are strongly discordant, so inverse-variance pooling masks a real conflict

name: synthesise_evidence
pre_conditions: A scalar RCT estimate+SE and a scalar (already transport-weighted) RWD estimate+SE
A chosen weight_mode ('inverse_variance' or 'rct_heavy') and, if transporting, a transport_shift and its SE

signature.description: Inverse-variance pooling of an RCT and RWD estimate with optional transport shift (Dahabreh et al. 2020; arXiv:2511.19735 2025). Validation: validated.

assumptions: Each input study estimate is approximately unbiased for its own population's effect with a known/consistent standard error
DerSimonian-Laird random-effects model: study effects are exchangeable draws from a common distribution with a single between-study variance tau^2
Within-study sampling errors are independent across studies and approximately normal
description: DerSimonian-Laird tau² / Q / I² heterogeneity statistics for multi-study evidence synthesis.

failure_modes.remedy: Pass equal-length sequences with strictly positive, finite standard errors for every study
failure_modes.symptom: estimates and ses have different lengths or contain non-positive / NaN SEs

failure_modes.remedy: Supply at least two independent study estimates before assessing heterogeneity
failure_modes.symptom: Only one study supplied, so tau^2 and Q have no degrees of freedom

name: heterogeneity_of_effect
pre_conditions: A sequence of point estimates plus a matching sequence of standard errors (same length, all SEs > 0)
At least two studies so a between-study variance is identifiable

signature.description: DerSimonian-Laird tau² / Q / I² heterogeneity statistics for multi-study evidence synthesis. Validation: validated.

assumptions: The RCT estimate is the unbiased benchmark target; concordance is judged relative to it
RWD and RCT estimate the same estimand for comparable (or successfully transported) populations
The RCT standard error is a valid measure of benchmark uncertainty at level alpha
description: Report-card: does the RWD estimate fall inside the RCT's 95% CI?

failure_modes.remedy: Provide a strictly positive finite RCT standard error and numeric point estimates
failure_modes.symptom: rct_se is zero, negative, or NaN so the concordance interval is undefined

failure_modes.remedy: Transport the RWD or RCT estimate to a common target population before comparing
failure_modes.symptom: RWD and RCT target different populations, so a real divergence is reported as discordance

name: rwd_rct_concordance
pre_conditions: A scalar RCT estimate with its SE plus a scalar RWD estimate to compare against it
Both estimates are on the same scale and target the same contrast

signature.description: Report-card: does the RWD estimate fall inside the RCT's 95% CI? Validation: validated.

assumptions: Correctness is decided by case-insensitive substring matching of the target answer in the LLM response — not semantic equivalence
The supplied llm_client is deterministic enough that scores are reproducible; randomness in sampling inflates score variance
Level-2 DAG-fragment questions presuppose ground-truth answers reflect a single agreed-upon causal structure
description: Level-1 (knowledge) and Level-2 (deductive reasoning) evaluation of an LLM's causal-reasoning ability.

failure_modes.remedy: Pass at least one item set with both question and answer columns.
failure_modes.symptom: neither level1_items nor level2_items supplied, or DataFrame lacks question/answer columns

failure_modes.remedy: Phrase target answers as short canonical tokens, or pre-normalize responses before scoring.
failure_modes.symptom: substring matching marks a semantically-correct paraphrase as wrong, deflating accuracy

At least one of level1_items / level2_items DataFrames with question and answer columns

assumptions: Every pair has a known ground-truth direction (A->B boolean); the task is binary causal-direction classification, not edge-presence discovery
The LLM's free-text reply is parsed for a yes/no signal via the prompt template — parsing fidelity caps measured accuracy
Pairs are treated as independent; shared-confounder pairs may have ill-defined ground truth

failure_modes.remedy: Set pair_a_col/pair_b_col/truth_col to columns that exist in the DataFrame.
failure_modes.symptom: named pair or truth column missing from ground_truth

failure_modes.remedy: Tighten prompt_template to force a yes/no token, or post-process responses before grading.
failure_modes.symptom: near-chance accuracy because replies do not fit the yes/no prompt template

assumptions: Overlap weights target the sub-population with positive overlap
Correct PS model OR outcome model for DR variant
Conditional parallel trends given covariates X after overlap weighting
Overlap (positivity): propensity score e(X) bounded away from 0 and 1 so overlap weights e(X)(1-e(X)) are well-behaved
No anticipation; correctly specified propensity model e(X) = P(treat=1 | X)
description: Overlap-weighted 2×2 DiD. Weights observations by e(X)(1-e(X)), where e(X) is the estimated propensity score, placing highest weight on units with the most overlap between treated and control covariate distributions. Useful when overlap is poor at the tails.

failure_modes.remedy: Trim extreme-PS units or simplify the covariate set to restore common support
failure_modes.symptom: Propensity scores pile up near 0/1 giving near-zero overlap weights and unstable ATT

failure_modes.remedy: Collapse to a single pre/post contrast or use a staggered/multi-period estimator
failure_modes.symptom: More than two time periods passed to this 2x2-only estimator

covariates with variation
Two-period panel with a binary treat indicator and a binary pre/post time indicator
Pre-treatment covariates supplied for the propensity score (without them it reduces to plain 2x2 DID)
Both treatment arms present in pre and post periods
reference: Li, Morgan & Zaslavsky (2018) JASA on overlap weights; applied to DiD by several authors [待核验 — specific citation to be confirmed].

assumptions: Standard MR instrument validity for the SNPs: relevance, independence (no confounding of SNP-outcome), and exclusion restriction acting only through exposure/mediator.
Two-step network-MR structure (Burgess et al. 2015): IVW for total and exposure->mediator effects, MVMR for the direct effect, with a linear/no-effect-modification mediation model.
Delta-method SE for the indirect effect treats the step components as combinable, assuming approximate normality of the IVW/MVMR estimates.
description: Two-step (network) MR: decompose the total causal effect of an exposure on an outcome into direct + indirect (mediated) components.

failure_modes.remedy: Add stronger independent instruments or check conditional F-statistics before running MVMR.
failure_modes.symptom: Weak or collinear instruments make the MVMR direct effect unidentified/unstable

failure_modes.remedy: Run pleiotropy-robust sensitivity (e.g., MR-Egger) and compare before trusting the decomposition.
failure_modes.symptom: Horizontal pleiotropy biases IVW total/direct effects

name: mr_mediation
pre_conditions: snp_associations has one row per SNP with beta+SE for exposure, mediator, and outcome.
Enough valid, non-overlapping instruments to identify the MVMR direct effect.
Exposure and mediator instruments are not collinear in the MVMR step.

signature.description: Two-step (network) MR: decompose the total causal effect of an exposure on an outcome into direct + indirect (mediated) components. Validation: validated.
signature.name: mr_mediation

assumptions: Random-effects pleiotropy: SNP-specific pleiotropic deviations are mean-zero with constant variance.
Measurement error in exposure betas follows a known shrinkage profile (RAPS bias correction).
Relevance: SNPs predict exposure (F-statistic ≥ 10 per SNP or set-F)
Independence: SNPs ⊥ confounders of exposure-outcome
Exclusion restriction: SNPs affect outcome only through exposure (InSIDE for Egger; ≥ 50% valid for median; modal for mode-based)
Monotonicity when interpreting LATE on genetically-shifted subpopulation
description: MR-RAPS: Robust Adjusted Profile Score for two-sample summary-data MR (Zhao et al. 2020, Annals of Statistics). Profile-likelihood MR with Tukey biweight loss + weak-instrument correction; resistant to a small fraction of gross pleiotropy outliers. Complements GRAPPLE (Gaussian) with a robust-loss variant of the same structural model.

failure_modes.remedy: Use Huber loss with fixed scale, or fall back to IVW with delta-method SEs.
failure_modes.symptom: Robust-loss tuning parameter does not stabilize

failure_modes.remedy: Use weighted-median or mode-based estimator; report Egger intercept + I² as pleiotropy diagnostic.
failure_modes.symptom: Egger intercept p < 0.05 — directional pleiotropy

failure_modes.remedy: Heterogeneity across SNPs — run sp.mr_presso to detect/remove outliers.
failure_modes.symptom: Q-statistic rejects homogeneity (Cochran's Q p < 0.05)

failure_modes.remedy: Weak-IV bias in IVW — use debiased IVW or LAP-type estimator (sp.mr_lap).
failure_modes.symptom: Set-F < 10 (weak instruments in aggregate)

failure_modes.remedy: SNPs explain more outcome variance than exposure — direction of effect questionable.
failure_modes.symptom: Steiger test flags reverse causation

name: mr_raps
pre_conditions: Many candidate instruments (>= 30 typical) with both strong and weaker SNPs available.
Two-sample summary data; SE columns must be present.
SNP-summary statistics for exposure and outcome aligned by SNP
beta_exposure / beta_outcome / se_exposure / se_outcome arrays of equal length
≥ 10 genetic instruments for reliable IVW/median/mode; ≥ 20 for robust Egger intercept
mvmr needs SNP × exposure associations matrix
reference: Zhao, Wang, Hemani, Bowden & Small (2020) Annals of Statistics 48(3).

signature.description: MR-RAPS: Robust Adjusted Profile Score for two-sample summary-data MR (Zhao et al. 2020, Annals of Statistics). Profile-likelihood MR with Tukey biweight loss + weak-instrument correction; resistant to a small fraction of gross pleiotropy outliers. Complements GRAPPLE (Gaussian) with a robust-loss variant of the same structural model. Validation: validated.
signature.name: mr_raps

assumptions: Contemporaneous structure W is acyclic (enforced via the NOTEARS trace-exponential constraint); lagged structure A may be dense
Stationary structural VAR with linear, additive relationships across the chosen lag window
Causal sufficiency for the contemporaneous part — no unobserved confounders driving simultaneous edges
Rows are evenly time-stamped and ordered; the SVAR treats observations as a single contiguous series
description: DYNOTEARS: continuous-optimisation structure learning for structural VARs. Returns contemporaneous (W) and lagged (A) adjacency matrices with the contemporaneous part enforced to be acyclic via the NOTEARS h(W) penalty.

failure_modes.remedy: Raise max_iter, loosen h_tol, or increase lambda_w to shrink spurious contemporaneous edges.
failure_modes.symptom: augmented-Lagrangian penalty hits rho_max without h(W) reaching h_tol; W not acyclic

failure_modes.remedy: Reduce lag or the number of variables, or supply a longer series.
failure_modes.symptom: too few time stamps relative to variables x (lag+1) yields an underdetermined, unstable fit

failure_modes.remedy: Lower threshold and/or the L1 penalties lambda_w/lambda_a.
failure_modes.symptom: all coefficients pruned to zero — empty graph

assumptions: Proximal surrogacy: an unobserved U may confound the surrogate-outcome link, relaxing conditional independence to two-stage completeness conditions (Imbens-Kallus-Mao-Wang 2025)
Valid proxies W: related to the unobserved confounder U and excluded from the direct effect on Y (IV-style exclusion), present in the observational sample only
Bridge-function identification holds; the linear-Gaussian 2SLS form assumes the bridge is linear
Comparability of the bridge/structure across the experimental and observational samples
description: Proximal surrogate-index estimator: long-term ATE when an unobserved U confounds S→Y, using a proxy W and 2SLS-style bridge-function identification (Imbens-Kallus-Mao-Wang 2025, JRSS-B).
example: sp.proximal_surrogate_index(exp, obs, treatment='T', surrogates=['s'], proxies=['w'], long_term_outcome='Y')

failure_modes.remedy: Supply proxies strongly related to U yet excluded from Y, ensuring the first stage is well-conditioned
failure_modes.symptom: Proxies are weak or fail the rank/completeness condition, so the 2SLS bridge is not identified

failure_modes.remedy: Use surrogate_index model hooks with a kernel/NN bridge and a custom 2SLS wrapper
failure_modes.symptom: Linear-Gaussian bridge is misspecified for a nonlinear data-generating process

failure_modes.remedy: Drop redundant proxies or regularise the first stage to restore conditioning
failure_modes.symptom: Near-collinear proxies make the 2SLS solution numerically unstable

name: proximal_surrogate_index
pre_conditions: Experimental sample with treatment and surrogates; observational sample with surrogates, proxies W, and the long-term outcome
At least as many proxies as needed to satisfy the completeness/rank condition for 2SLS
Proxies measured only in the observational sample
reference: Imbens, Kallus, Mao & Wang (2025). JRSS-B 87(2), 362-388. arXiv:2202.07234.

failure_modes.symptom: Two-way fixed-effects estimate is contaminated by 'forbidden' comparisons / negative weights

description: Long-panel Double/Debiased ML for static panel models with fixed effects (Clarke & Polselli 2025, simplified). Absorbs unit (and optional time) fixed effects via within-transform, cross-fits ML nuisance learners with folds that split units, and reports cluster-robust SE at the unit level. failure_modes.symptom: Within-unit variation in treatment is near zero

failure_modes.remedy: ML nuisances not learnable on within-transformed data; use sp.panel FE or richer features.
failure_modes.symptom: Nuisance cross-val R² near zero on demeaned outcomes

failure_modes.remedy: Cluster-robust SE handles within-unit correlation, but report Driscoll-Kraay (sp.panel robust='driscoll-kraay') if cross-sectional dependence likely.

covariates are time-varying (pure time-invariant ones get absorbed by unit FE)

description: Synthetic Survival Control (Han & Shah 2025, arXiv:2511.14133). Fits a convex combination of donor Kaplan-Meier curves on the complementary log-log scale to match the treated arm's pre-treatment survival, then reports the post-treatment survival gap with placebo UCBs.

failure_modes.remedy: Poor pre-fit — switch to method='demeaned'/'augmented' or enlarge donor pool.

assumptions: The two estimation paths being bridged target the same causal estimand under the chosen bridging theorem (e.g., DID = synthetic control for kind='did_sc').
Each path's own identification holds (parallel trends / SC convex-hull / proximal completeness, depending on kind), so a non-rejected agreement test is meaningful.
The doubly-robust combined estimate is consistent if at least one of the two bridged paths is correctly specified.
description: Unified dispatcher for six causal-inference bridging theorems (2025-2026): DiD≡SC (Shi-Athey), EWM≡CATE (Ferman), IPW≡DR≡CB (Zhao-Percival), Bunching≡RDD (Lu-Wang-Xie), DR-via-Calibration (Zhang), Long-term-surrogate≡PCI (Imbens-Kallus-Mao-Wang). Reports both path estimates + doubly-robust recommendation.
example: sp.bridge(df, kind="did_sc", y="wage", group="state", time="year", first_treat="g")

failure_modes.remedy: Treat divergence as evidence one bridging assumption fails and diagnose each path separately rather than trusting the DR combination.
failure_modes.symptom: Agreement test rejects: the two paths give materially different estimates

failure_modes.remedy: Pass a supported kind and the exact keyword arguments documented for that bridge.
failure_modes.symptom: Unknown kind or missing bridge-specific kwargs

name: bridge

reference: Sun-Xie-Zhang (2503.11375); Ferman et al. (2510.26723); Zhao-Percival (2310.18563); Lu-Wang-Xie (2404.09117); Zhang et al. (2411.02771); Imbens-Kallus-Mao-Wang (2202.07234, JRSS-B 2025).

alternatives: sp.regress with raw LLM label (biased — for comparison only)
assumptions: Misclassification is non-differential: T_obs ⫫ y | T_true
Validation subset is representative of the full sample

description: [experimental] Egami et al. (2024) measurement-error correction for downstream OLS coefficients when the treatment indicator was produced by an LLM (or any imperfect classifier). Binary T uses Hausman (1998) 1/(1 - p_01 - p_10) inflation; multi-class T (K>=3) uses the inverse-confusion-matrix transform built from the validation-set Bayes posterior. Optional bias-corrected bootstrap jointly resamples the validation set and the unlabeled corpus for honest CIs. SE inflation factor (delta-method) always reported in diagnostics.

failure_modes.remedy: Hand-label more rows so that annotations_human has >=30 non-NaN entries spanning every class
failure_modes.symptom: DataInsufficient: 'At least 30 validation rows'

failure_modes.remedy: Misclassification too severe — re-prompt the LLM or hand-label more
failure_modes.symptom: IdentificationFailure: '1-p_01-p_10 <= 0' or transform matrix is near-singular

failure_modes.remedy: Increase n_bootstrap, or fall back to the first-order SE; resampling is too unstable when the validation set is very small
failure_modes.symptom: DataInsufficient: 'Bootstrap produced only N valid draws'

limitations: method='hausman' is the only supported correction; the logistic and Bayesian variants from Egami et al. (2024) are not yet implemented

assumptions: A bootstrap SIR (sequential importance resampling) filter is appropriate: the latent causal parameter follows a (possibly non-Gaussian) random-walk transition with process_sd and the supplied models.
The observation log-pdf correctly links each period's estimate and standard_error to the latent state (e.g., Gaussian measurement with the given SE).
n_particles is large enough to approximate the filtering distribution, and systematic resampling at the ESS threshold curbs weight degeneracy.
description: Bootstrap SIR particle filter for non-Gaussian assimilative causal inference.  Supports arbitrary prior sampler, transition sampler, and observation log-pdf, with systematic resampling triggered by an ESS threshold.

failure_modes.remedy: Increase n_particles, raise process_sd, or lower the ESS resample threshold to trigger resampling earlier.
failure_modes.symptom: Particle weights degenerate (ESS collapses) so the posterior is driven by one particle

failure_modes.remedy: Align the two sequences and drop/repair NaN or non-positive SE entries before filtering.
failure_modes.symptom: estimates and standard_errors differ in length or contain non-finite values

name: particle_filter
pre_conditions: estimates and standard_errors are aligned, finite, equal-length sequences over time.
prior_mean/prior_var/process_sd specify a coherent state-space prior and dynamics.
n_particles and ess_resample_threshold set sensibly.

signature.description: Bootstrap SIR particle filter for non-Gaussian assimilative causal inference.  Supports arbitrary prior sampler, transition sampler, and observation log-pdf, with systematic resampling triggered by an ESS threshold. Validation: validated.

assumptions: Relevance: SNPs predict exposure (F-statistic ≥ 10 per SNP or set-F)
Independence: SNPs ⊥ confounders of exposure-outcome
Exclusion restriction: SNPs affect outcome only through exposure (InSIDE for Egger; ≥ 50% valid for median; modal for mode-based)
Monotonicity when interpreting LATE on genetically-shifted subpopulation
description: Unified Mendelian Randomization dispatcher. method= selects the estimator: 'ivw' / 'egger' / 'median' / 'penalized_median' / 'mode' / 'all' (runs IVW+Egger+Median together) / 'mvmr' / 'mediation' / 'bma' (multi-exposure) / 'presso' / 'radial' / 'leave_one_out' / 'steiger' / 'heterogeneity' / 'pleiotropy_egger' / 'f_statistic' (diagnostics).  Kwargs are passed through to the target function unchanged; see sp.mendelian_family guide.

failure_modes.remedy: Use weighted-median or mode-based estimator; report Egger intercept + I² as pleiotropy diagnostic.
failure_modes.symptom: Egger intercept p < 0.05 — directional pleiotropy

failure_modes.remedy: Heterogeneity across SNPs — run sp.mr_presso to detect/remove outliers.
failure_modes.symptom: Q-statistic rejects homogeneity (Cochran's Q p < 0.05)

failure_modes.remedy: Weak-IV bias in IVW — use debiased IVW or LAP-type estimator (sp.mr_lap).
failure_modes.symptom: Set-F < 10 (weak instruments in aggregate)

failure_modes.remedy: SNPs explain more outcome variance than exposure — direction of effect questionable.
failure_modes.symptom: Steiger test flags reverse causation

name: mr
pre_conditions: SNP-summary statistics for exposure and outcome aligned by SNP
beta_exposure / beta_outcome / se_exposure / se_outcome arrays of equal length
≥ 10 genetic instruments for reliable IVW/median/mode; ≥ 20 for robust Egger intercept
mvmr needs SNP × exposure associations matrix
reference: Burgess et al. 2013; Bowden et al. 2015/2016/2017/2018; Verbanck et al. 2018; Hartwig et al. 2017; Sanderson et al. 2019; Zuber et al. 2020.

signature.description: Unified Mendelian Randomization dispatcher. method= selects the estimator: 'ivw' / 'egger' / 'median' / 'penalized_median' / 'mode' / 'all' (runs IVW+Egger+Median together) / 'mvmr' / 'mediation' / 'bma' (multi-exposure) / 'presso' / 'radial' / 'leave_one_out' / 'steiger' / 'heterogeneity' / 'pleiotropy_egger' / 'f_statistic' (diagnostics). For kind='interference': cluster-exchangeable exchangeability
description: Unified conformal causal inference dispatcher. kind= selects the estimator: 'cate' / 'counterfactual' / 'ite' (Lei-Candès 2021 base) / 'weighted' (TBCR 2019 primitive) / 'density' / 'multidp' / 'debiased' / 'fair' (2025-2026 frontier) / 'continuous' (dose-response) / 'interference' (cluster-exchangeable).  Kwargs pass through to the target function; see sp.conformal_family guide.
example: sp.conformal("cate", data=df, y="y", treat="d", covariates=["x1", "x2"], alpha=0.1)

failure_modes.remedy: Use kind='weighted' with estimated density ratios.
failure_modes.symptom: Calibration and test distributions differ (covariate shift)

failure_modes.remedy: Increase calibration sample or raise alpha; coverage gets loose below ~100.
failure_modes.symptom: Calibration set too small — intervals wide

failure_modes.remedy: Use kind='debiased' which orthogonalises via DML-style nuisance handling.
failure_modes.symptom: Miscalibrated nuisance (propensity / outcome) for CATE/ITE

exchangeability between calibration and test distributions (weighted variants for covariate shift)

≥ 500 calibration observations for reliable finite-sample coverage at alpha ≤ 0.1

Kwargs pass through to the target function; see sp.interference_family guide.

failure_modes.symptom: Exposure mapping misspecified

assumptions: Parallel trends in potential outcomes across dose levels
No anticipation of treatment
Strong parallel trends (CGS 2024) required for ATT(d|g,t) interpretation in att_gt mode
Overlap: positive density of dose in the treated support
description: DiD with continuous treatment intensity. Four modes: (i) 'twfe' TWFE with dose×post interaction; (ii) 'att_gt' dose-quantile group-time ATT versus the untreated (dose=0) arm with bootstrap SE (heuristic); (iii) 'dose_response' local-linear regression of ΔY=Y_post−Y_pre on baseline dose; (iv) 'cgs' Callaway-Goodman-Bacon-Sant'Anna (2024) ATT(d|g,t) MVP — 2-period design, OR only, bootstrap SE, [待核验] markers on paper formulas. Full CGS parity (cohort aggregation, DR/IPW, analytical IF variance) is on the roadmap — see docs/rfc/continuous_did_cgs.md.
example: sp.continuous_did(df, y="wage", dose="training_hours", time="year", id="worker_id", t_pre=2019, t_post=2020)

failure_modes.remedy: Lowest-dose quantile is auto-used as the control arm; pass an explicit never-treated indicator via post= if this is not intended.
failure_modes.symptom: No units with dose == 0

failure_modes.remedy: Not enough variation in dose. Lower n_quantiles, or check that dose is truly continuous in the baseline period.
failure_modes.symptom: Dose collapses to one quantile

failure_modes.remedy: Current modes use bootstrap / OLS SE. The CGS 2024 analytical IF is tracked in docs/rfc/continuous_did_cgs.md; until landed, inflate n_boot or cluster bootstrap manually.
failure_modes.symptom: SE appears too small — you want the CGS 2024 analytical influence-function variance

limitations: method='cgs' is an MVP — 2-period design, OR only, bootstrap SE; full CGS parity (cohort aggregation, DR/IPW, analytical IF variance) is on the roadmap (see docs/rfc/continuous_did_cgs.md). Other modes (twfe / att_gt / dose_response) are stable.
name: continuous_did
pre_conditions: panel data with unit × time × outcome × continuous dose
at least one unit with dose == 0 acts as untreated control (or the lowest dose quantile is used as control)
both a pre and a post period per unit
reference: Callaway, Goodman-Bacon & Sant'Anna (2024) [@callaway2024difference]; de Chaisemartin & D'Haultfœuille (2018) [@dechaisemartin2018fuzzy].

Full CGS parity (cohort aggregation, DR/IPW, analytical IF variance) is on the roadmap — see docs/rfc/continuous_did_cgs.md. Validation: validated. alpha.description: Significance level for confidence intervals and tests.

cluster.description: Cluster variable for SE (TWFE mode)

data.description: pandas DataFrame containing the variables used by the estimator.

method.description: Estimation mode

n_quantiles.description: Number of dose quantiles for discretisation

post.description: Binary post-treatment indicator (inferred from t_pre / t_post if omitted)

y.description: Outcome variable

assumptions: Parallel trends between switchers and stayers
Stable treatment effects across consecutive periods (for the DID_M weighted average interpretation)
No anticipation
Cluster-bootstrap validity requires G large and clusters independent
Parallel trends: treated and control groups would have followed the same trajectory absent treatment
No anticipation: outcomes in pre-treatment periods are unaffected by future treatment
SUTVA: no spillovers between units
For staggered / heterogeneous effects: use CS or SA — TWFE can produce negative weights (Goodman-Bacon)
description: de Chaisemartin & D'Haultfœuille (2020) DID_M estimator. Weighted average of consecutive-period DID cells where treatment 'switchers' are compared to 'stayers'. Handles treatments that switch on AND off (unlike Callaway-Sant'Anna which assumes staggered adoption). Supports placebo lags, dynamic horizons, cluster bootstrap SE, joint placebo test and average-cumulative-effect summary from dCDH (2024). The heteroskedastic-weights variant and full dCDH (2024) intertemporal event-study (did_multiplegt_dyn Stata) are on the roadmap — see docs/rfc/multiplegt_dyn.md.
example: sp.did_multiplegt(df, y="wage", group="county", time="year", treatment="treated", placebo=2, dynamic=3, cluster="state", n_boot=200, seed=42)

failure_modes.remedy: did_multiplegt identifies effects only from treatment switches. Fall back to callaway_santanna if the design is staggered adoption.
failure_modes.symptom: No switching cells (nobody changes treatment)

failure_modes.remedy: Inspect model_info['event_study'] by placebo lag; consider honest_did sensitivity bounds or add controls.
failure_modes.symptom: Joint placebo test rejects — parallel trends unlikely

failure_modes.remedy: Raise n_boot, or switch to a wild cluster bootstrap. The analytical influence-function SE from dCDH (2020) is not yet implemented — see RFC.
failure_modes.symptom: Bootstrap SE unstable with small G

failure_modes.remedy: Use sp.sensitivity_rr (Rambachan & Roth honest CI) or switch to sp.callaway_santanna.
failure_modes.symptom: Pre-trend joint test p < 0.05 (or underpowered at 0.10)

failure_modes.remedy: TWFE can give negative weights; use Callaway-Sant'Anna, Sun-Abraham, or BJS imputation.
failure_modes.symptom: Staggered treatment timing with TWFE method

failure_modes.remedy: Check sp.pretrends_power — if low, report honest CI via sp.sensitivity_rr.
failure_modes.symptom: Pre-trend test underpowered (Roth 2022)

failure_modes.remedy: Use wild cluster bootstrap (sp.wild_cluster_bootstrap).
failure_modes.symptom: Few clusters at unit level

name: did_multiplegt
pre_conditions: long-format panel with one row per unit × period
treatment is binary (0/1) and may vary over time within a unit
at least two periods observed per unit so a first difference can be computed
data is panel or repeated cross-section with a time column
treat column is binary (0/1) for 2x2, or first-treatment-period (int) for staggered
at least one pre-treatment period (≥ 2 periods for 2x2; ≥ 3 recommended for event study)
for staggered designs: id column identifying units across time
reference: de Chaisemartin & D'Haultfœuille (2020) [@dechaisemartin2020two]; 2022 survey [@dechaisemartin2022fixed]; 2024 joint placebo + avg cumulative [@dechaisemartin2024difference].

Handles treatments that switch on AND off (unlike Callaway-Sant'Anna which assumes staggered adoption). No anticipation within window
